multi_agent_system_builder_workflow.overview: This file defines the complete generation workflow for multi-agent-system-builder mode. This mode reads implementation.md and generates all backend files for a local multi-agent system using FastAPI + LangGraph + ChatOllama (langchain-ollama). It NEVER executes commands — it generates scripts and provides clear run instructions. It NEVER starts without implementation.md being present and valid.

core_principles.principle: ALWAYS read implementation.md before generating a single file. If implementation.md does not exist, tell the user to run deep-plan mode first. If it exists but is missing required sections, list the gaps and ask the user to complete it before proceeding.
NEVER run deployment or setup commands directly. Always generate scripts (setup.sh, start.sh, etc.) with clear, annotated instructions for the user to run manually.

Generate files in dependency order. Never generate a file that imports from a file that has not been generated yet in this session. Order: config → models → llm → tools → graph → API → workflows → scripts
Present the generation plan before starting. After generating each major component, pause briefly to confirm the user wants to continue to the next component.
All agent names, tool names, module names, function names, file names: snake_case. No exceptions in Python. React components are the only exception (PascalCase).

BEFORE generating any LangGraph, LangChain, or ChatOllama code, you MUST complete a full MCP research session (Phase 0, Step 4). This is non-negotiable. MCP provides current API syntax. Rule files provide architecture patterns and quality standards. When they conflict on syntax, MCP always wins. Never write a constructor parameter, import path, or method signature from a rule file without first verifying it is current via MCP results.

All code shown in rule files is STRUCTURAL GUIDANCE — it illustrates architecture shape and intent, not guaranteed current syntax. Any code that imports from langchain, langgraph, or langchain_ollama must be MCP-verified before being written into a generated file. Treat rule file code as a blueprint, not a ready-to-copy template.

Before generating any file, check if a version already exists in the project. If backend/app/ already contains code, read it first — generate targeted additions or diffs, not full replacements that would destroy existing work.

step.if_not_found: Tell the user: "implementation.md not found. Please run deep-plan mode first to generate the architectural plan before switching to this mode." Do not proceed.

step.if_sections_missing: List exactly which sections are missing and what information they should contain. Ask the user to complete them. Do not generate partial output.

step.output_format: Present a todo list of all files to be generated, grouped by component. Ask: "This is the full generation plan based on implementation.md. Shall I proceed?"

step.action: Complete MCP research session — before writing the first line of generated code
step.description: Run all baseline queries plus topology-specific queries based on implementation.md. Read and confirm results. Present a brief API confirmation summary to the user. Do NOT proceed to Phase 1 without completing this step. This is the single most important guard against generating code with outdated syntax.
step.token_budget_note: Run these queries ONCE in Phase 0. All downstream rule files (2–8) reference these results — they do NOT re-query. This is the only MCP batch for the entire session, except for the one topology query and the streaming query if streaming is required.

baseline_queries.query: search_docs_by_lang_chain("LangGraph StateGraph MessagesState ainvoke config thread_id compile MemorySaver import current")
search_docs_by_lang_chain("ChatOllama langchain-ollama constructor parameters base_url temperature num_ctx keep_alive import current")
search_docs_by_lang_chain("LangGraph create_react_agent prebuilt prompt state_modifier system message signature current")
search_docs_by_lang_chain("LangChain tool decorator @tool async langchain-core import current")
search_docs_by_lang_chain("LangChain HumanMessage AIMessage SystemMessage langchain-core import current")

topology_queries.comment: Run ONLY the one matching implementation.md Orchestration Topology. Skip the rest.
topology_queries.query: search_docs_by_lang_chain("LangGraph supervisor add_conditional_edges routing END current")
search_docs_by_lang_chain("LangGraph sequential pipeline add_edge START END current")
search_docs_by_lang_chain("LangGraph Send API Annotated operator.add reducer map reduce parallel current")

query.note: Also run this if streaming endpoint is required by implementation.md

topology_queries.query: search_docs_by_lang_chain("LangGraph astream_events streaming version v2 on_chat_model_stream current")

step.output_format: After completing queries, present a brief confirmation before starting Phase 1: "MCP research complete. Confirmed for this session: - StateGraph/MessagesState import: [from MCP] - MemorySaver import + ainvoke config thread_id structure: [from MCP] - ChatOllama import + confirmed parameter names: [from MCP] - create_react_agent signature + system prompt parameter: [from MCP] - @tool import: [from MCP] - HumanMessage/AIMessage/SystemMessage import: [from MCP] These results cover all downstream files. No further MCP queries needed unless production persistence (SqliteSaver) or streaming is implemented."

file.purpose: Pydantic Settings class for all environment variables with defaults

content_rules.rule: Use pydantic-settings BaseSettings with SettingsConfigDict
All env vars from implementation.md provider_configuration table
Include OLLAMA_BASE_URL, DEFAULT_MODEL, LLM_TEMPERATURE, OLLAMA_NUM_CTX, OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE as minimum
Add one per-agent model field per agent from implementation.md Agent Inventory
Export a single settings singleton: settings = Settings()
See 8_code_generation.xml config_template

file.purpose: Pydantic API models — ChatRequest, ChatResponse, TaskResponse, TaskResult

content_rules.rule: Only API boundary models go here — LangGraph manages internal state
See 8_code_generation.xml schemas_template

file.purpose: Python dependencies — LangGraph stack

content_rules.rule: Must include: fastapi, uvicorn, pydantic, pydantic-settings, httpx, langchain-core, langchain-ollama, langgraph
Do NOT include the raw ollama package — langchain-ollama wraps it
See 8_code_generation.xml requirements_template

file.purpose: get_llm() factory returning configured ChatOllama instances

content_rules.rule: See 4_provider_ollama.xml get_llm_factory for the structural pattern
Use Phase 0 Step 4 MCP results for confirmed parameter names — do not re-query
Uses langchain_ollama.ChatOllama — NOT langchain_community
Reads all parameters from settings (never hardcoded)

phase.name: tools
phase.title: LangChain Tools
phase.condition: Only generate if implementation.md Agent Inventory defines tool-using agents

file.purpose: @tool decorated functions for a given domain (web, database, file, etc.)

content_rules.rule: See 3_agent_design.xml tool_pattern for the structural pattern
Use Phase 0 Step 4 MCP results for confirmed @tool import — do not re-query
Docstrings are critical — the LLM reads them to decide when to call the tool
Never raise exceptions from tools — catch errors and return a string

file.purpose: State type definitions — MessagesState extensions for supervisor or routing patterns

content_rules.rule: See 2_orchestration_core.xml state_types and 8_code_generation.xml graph_state_template
Only add fields beyond MessagesState when routing metadata is needed

file.purpose: All graph node functions — one per agent from implementation.md Agent Inventory

content_rules.rule: See 3_agent_design.xml for react_agent_pattern and custom_node_pattern
Use Phase 0 Step 4 MCP results for confirmed create_react_agent signature — do not re-query
ReAct agents use create_react_agent() from langgraph.prebuilt
Custom nodes are async def functions returning dict state updates
System prompts from implementation.md Agent Inventory entries
Model assigned via get_llm(model=settings.X_model) — never hardcoded

file.purpose: StateGraph construction and compilation — the orchestration definition

content_rules.rule: See 2_orchestration_core.xml for supervisor_pattern and pipeline_pattern
Use Phase 0 Step 4 MCP results for confirmed StateGraph/MemorySaver imports — do not re-query
Pattern from implementation.md Orchestration Topology section: supervisor-worker or pipeline
Returns compiled graph: builder.compile(checkpointer=MemorySaver())
WORKER_AGENTS list and conditional_edges routing map must be in sync

file.purpose: FastAPI application entry point — graph compilation in lifespan

content_rules.rule: See 8_code_generation.xml fastapi_app_template
Lifespan: check Ollama, warm up model, compile graph, store in app.state.graph
CORS middleware configured for frontend origin

file.purpose: All API endpoint definitions from implementation.md API Design section

content_rules.rule: See 8_code_generation.xml api_routes_template
Routes call req.app.state.graph.ainvoke() with thread_id=request.session_id
Always include /health endpoint that checks Ollama connectivity
Include streaming endpoint if implementation.md requires it (see 6_workflow_patterns.xml streaming)

phase.name: workflows
phase.title: Additional Workflow Implementations
phase.condition: Only generate if implementation.md defines complex multi-step workflows beyond the main graph

file.purpose: Alternative graph definition for a specific workflow variant

content_rules.rule: See 6_workflow_patterns.xml for pipeline, parallel, conditional, and streaming patterns

file.purpose: Backend container — Python 3.11-slim, installs requirements.txt, runs uvicorn

content_rules.rule: See 8_code_generation.xml backend_dockerfile_template
COPY requirements.txt before COPY . for Docker layer caching
CMD uses --reload for development; remove for production

file.purpose: Frontend container — Node 20-slim, installs npm deps, runs Vite dev server

content_rules.rule: See 8_code_generation.xml frontend_dockerfile_template
Must bind to 0.0.0.0 so Docker port mapping works: npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0

file.purpose: Orchestrates backend + frontend containers. Single command to start everything.

content_rules.rule: See 8_code_generation.xml docker_compose_template
extra_hosts host.docker.internal:host-gateway is required on Linux for Ollama access
Backend reads from backend/.env via env_file
Volume mounts enable live reload without rebuild

file.purpose: Prevents .env, __pycache__, .venv from entering the Docker image

content_rules.rule: See 8_code_generation.xml dockerignore_template

file.purpose: Template environment file — all variables from implementation.md, no real values

content_rules.rule: See 8_code_generation.xml env_example_template
All variables from config.py Settings class
Include all per-agent model overrides, commented out

file.purpose: Prevent secrets and build artifacts from git — especially .env

content_rules.rule: See 8_code_generation.xml gitignore_template
Always include .env, .venv/, __pycache__/, *.pyc, checkpoints.db

file.purpose: Project documentation — setup, running, env vars, how to add agents

content_rules.rule: See 8_code_generation.xml readme_template as baseline
Replace placeholder names and env vars with actual values from implementation.md
Include all agents in the system and their purpose
"How to Add a New Agent" section must reflect the LangGraph node pattern

file.purpose: pytest configuration — asyncio_mode = "auto" is required for async tests to run

content_rules.rule: See 8_code_generation.xml pyproject_template
Without asyncio_mode = "auto", async test functions silently pass without executing

file.purpose: Shared pytest fixtures — mock_llm fixture available to all test files

content_rules.rule: See 8_code_generation.xml conftest_template
mock_llm patches get_llm() — used in node unit tests

file.purpose: Unit test stubs for graph nodes — one test per node minimum

content_rules.rule: See 7_best_practices.xml mock_llm_pattern for the test structure
One test for happy path, one for error path per node
Use patch("app.graph.nodes.get_llm") to inject the mock_llm fixture

step.action: List all generated files with their paths

checklist_items.item: Copy backend/.env.example to backend/.env and set OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434
Pull required Ollama models on the host: ollama pull MODEL_NAME (one per model in implementation.md)
Run: docker compose up --build — wait for both containers to be healthy
Run /lint on generated files in a new Advanced mode session

step.action: Tell the user what to do next
step.message: "All backend files have been generated. Next steps: 1. Edit backend/.env — set OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434 and tune OLLAMA_NUM_CTX for your hardware 2. Pull required Ollama models on your host: ollama pull MODEL_NAME 3. Run: docker compose up --build from the project root 4. Open a new session in Advanced mode and run /lint to catch any issues 5. When ready for the frontend, switch to frontend-craftsman mode"
